channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: ING da más peso en su organización al negocio de Seguros
item.description: La nueva organización otorga mayor peso estratégico a los seguros dentro de la estructura de ING y refuerza su presencia en el comité de banca retail. ING ha reorganizado su banca retail en España y, para impulsar el crecimiento del negocio asegurador , crea un área independiente. Al frente de la nueva área se sitúa Gonzalo Caselles , que asume la responsabilidad tras haber trabajado previamente en el desarrollo del negocio asegurador dentro del banco.

item.title: Grup Coyfer integra la correduría AFIS
item.description: Grup Coyfer ha integrado la correduría de seguros AFIS en una operación destinada a reforzar su capacidad operativa . Con esta incorporación, AFIS pasa a formar parte de la estructura de Coyfer , «sumándose a un proyecto con mayor capacidad técnica, recursos especializados y una organización operativa más amplia», se destaca en el anuncio. La integración se enmarca en la estrategia de crecimiento y consolidación del grupo dentro del mercado.

item.title: Trust but Verify: Why Insurers Should Embrace Stronger Governance
item.description: Traditional AI capabilities such as machine learning often produce deterministic outcomes; they’ve earned the trust of insurers and other financial institutions because their outputs are typically auditable, traceable, and repeatable. First, it provides a path to “provide regulators with access to third-party data and models used in insurance functions with direct consumer impact and to establish governance standards for model and data integrity, consumer protection, and ongoing monitoring.” The framework would also require insurers to register models with the appropriate state department of insurance before using them. Finally, “a senior officer must attest that the governance program is implemented, effective, and applied to all models with direct consumer impact used by licensed insurers [and] complies with applicable insurance laws and regulations.” For now, the framework remains within the workgroup and has not been adopted.

item.title: Bessent, Powell Warned Bank CEOs About Anthropic Model Risks, Sources Say
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell convened an urgent meeting with bank CEOs this week to warn of cyber risks posed by Anthropic’s latest artificial intelligence model, two sources familiar with the matter said on Thursday. government officials about the model’s “offensive and defensive cyber capabilities.” A third source close to the matter reiterated Anthropic’s outreach, saying the company proactively briefed senior U.S. He brought them over to the Treasury, along with Jay Powell, and went through the cyber risks to make sure that they were aware of them,” Hassett said, adding that the Trump administration is taking steps to ensure the public is safe from potential risks.

item.title: Hyundai to Recall Over 294,000 US Vehicles Over Seat Belt Anchor Defect
item.description: due to a defect that could cause seat belt anchors to detach, the U.S. Hyundai Motor America is recalling 294,128 vehicles in the U.S. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said on Friday.

item.title: Abbott Infant-Formula Jury Awards $70 Million Damages
item.description: Abbott Laboratories was told by a jury to pay $70 million over claims by four mothers that the company hid the fact its premature-infant formula can cause a bowel disease dangerous to frail babies. After awarding the mothers $53 million in compensation for their losses on Thursday, the jury on Friday imposed $17 million in punitive damages on Abbott. In brief arguments Friday morning, an attorney for the plaintiffs urged jurors to award a combined $530 million in punitive damages, saying it would take a significant sum to incentivize Abbott to protect the most vulnerable newborns going forward.

item.title: India firms up plans for GIC Re-led US$100m marine war risk pool
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: APAC insurers brace for indirect impact of Middle East conflict as volatility, supply-chain risks rise: S&P Global
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Victoria bushfire losses rise to US$570m: Perils
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Best of Artemis, week ending April 12th 2026
item.description: Ten most read articles on Artemis.bm, week ending April 12th 2026: This is not every article published on Artemis during the last week, just the most popular among our readers over the last seven days. Here are the ten most popular news articles, week ending April 12th 2026, covering catastrophe bonds, ILS, reinsurance capital and related risk transfer topics. To ensure you never miss a thing subscribe to the weekly Artemis email newsletter updates or get our email alerts for every article we publish.

item.title: Florida Peninsula secures upsized $250m Palm Re 2026-1 cat bond at reduced pricing
item.description: Now, we can report that Florida Peninsula successfully finalised its third cat bond sponsorship to provide the two-thirds upsized $250 million of multi-year and collateralized reinsurance, while the pricing was finalised at a below guidance level. Once this new 2026-1 issuance has settled, Florida Peninsula will have $650 million of Florida named storm reinsurance outstanding for the upcoming hurricane season from its Palm Re program of catastrophe bond deals . Florida Peninsula Insurance Company has now priced and secured the two-thirds upsized target of $250 million of named storm reinsurance from its new Palm Re Ltd.

item.title: Euclid Transactional announces 21 senior promotions
item.description: Euclid Transactional, a managing general agent (MGA) specialising in transactional insurance underwriting and claims handling, has announced 21 senior-level promotions across various global regions and functions. “Each of the 21 senior individuals promoted today is a leader in the transactional insurance industry, and we are proud of the service and knowledge they provide to our clients and carriers across the globe.” Kit Westropp, Managing Principal, EMEA, added, “It has been incredible to see the development of our team over this past year and to see so many colleagues developing their careers at Euclid, these promotions are a real testament to the talent, commitment and ambition within our team. These 21 promotions form part of 47 title and role changes, spanning Euclid Transactional’s global claims, operations, and underwriting teams.

item.title: Gartner forecasts surge in AI-related legal claims and urges stronger insurance strategies
item.description: Gartner, a research and advisory company specialising in business and technology insights, has projected that more than 2,000 legal claims linked to so-called “death by AI” incidents will be brought worldwide by the end of 2026. According to Gartner, General Counsel should begin incorporating dedicated AI insurance products into their wider risk management frameworks. In Gartner’s view, this development will also influence how accountability is assigned within organisations, with those demonstrating mature governance practices better positioned to secure favourable insurance terms and reassure stakeholders.

item.title: Pool Re launches new SME incentive scheme to increase terrorism cover uptake
item.description: Pool Re, Britain’s largest terrorism reinsurer, has launched a new incentive scheme for its Members, effective 1 April, offering discounts on reinsurance costs when they reintegrate terrorism cover as standard across SME property policies. This scheme gives our Members an additional incentive to offer terrorism cover as standard, as was the case prior to the formation of Pool Re in 1993. “Member participation in the scheme is entirely voluntary, but to qualify, terrorism cover must be embedded as standard in the product and non-removable from policies, helping to ensure that take-up delivers meaningful protection for smaller businesses,” Pool Re observed.

item.title: Worsening Ocean Heat Waves Are ‘Supercharging’ Hurricane Damage, Study Finds
item.description: Marine heat waves are supercharging damage caused by hurricanes and tropical cyclones across the globe, a new study found. The study defined marine heat waves as long-lasting, large areas of water in the top 10% of historical heat. “These marine heat waves affect more than half of landfalling tropical cyclones,” said study co-author Gregory Foltz, an oceanographer at the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration.

item.title: OpenAI Identifies Security Issue Involving Third-Party Tool Axios
item.description: OpenAI said on April 10 it had identified a security issue involving a third-party developer tool called Axios and is taking steps to protect the process that certifies its macOS applications are legitimate OpenAI apps. Passwords and OpenAI API keys were not affected by the third-party security issue, the company said, adding that the root cause of the security incident was a misconfiguration in the GitHub Actions workflow, which has been addressed. According to OpenAI, Axios, a widely used third-party developer library, was compromised on March 31, as part of a broader software supply chain attack by actors believed to be linked to North Korea.

item.title: Trump Approves Disaster Requests for at Least 7 States; Others Wait
item.description: The Trump administration approved major disaster declaration requests for at least seven states this week, according to information released Saturday by the Federal Emergency Management Agency, allowing affected communities to access federal support. “We’re trying to push this stuff forward as fast as possible,” Mullin said after surveying Hurricane Helene recovery work in North Carolina on his first official visit as DHS secretary, acknowledging that “disasters are happening constantly.” White House spokeswoman Abigail Jackson said Saturday that Trump responds to such requests “with great care and consideration, ensuring American tax dollars are used appropriately and efficiently by the states to supplement — not substitute — their obligation to respond to and recover from disasters.” She said an administration goal is having state and local governments “invest in their own resilience before disaster strikes, making response less urgent and recovery less prolonged.” While Mullin assured fellow senators during his confirmation hearing that he believed in FEMA’s mission, the agency’s future is uncertain. About 15 requests for assistance from others states and tribes for extreme weather events this year and last seem to be pending, along with three appeals of previous denials.

item.title: Findevor Expands Portfolio Intelligence Platform with Claims Intelligence Capability
item.description: The post Findevor Expands Portfolio Intelligence Platform with Claims Intelligence Capability appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . New capability helps underwriting leadership identify emerging loss patterns earlier by combining structured loss information with unstructured claims data New York, NY – April 13, 2026 Findevor announced the development of a Claims Intelligence product within its Portfolio Intelligence operating layer, designed to help underwriting leadership identify emerging loss patterns and performance signals earlier by translating claims activity into decision-relevant portfolio insight. “Claims Intelligence is an important step in completing the platform across the full portfolio management lifecycle, spanning distribution, underwriting, and claims,” said Virgil Tataru, Co-Founder and CTO of Findevor.

item.title: As Blueprint Two heads off into the sunset, what next for Lloyd’s transformation?
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: The Standard partners with the University of Texas at Dallas to increase access to the actuarial science program
item.description: The post The Standard partners with the University of Texas at Dallas to increase access to the actuarial science program appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . The Standard announced a new partnership with the University of Texas at Dallas to provide a scholarship for students pursuing a degree in actuarial science who need financial assistance. “The Standard Scholarship for Actuarial Science at UT Dallas is an important investment in the future.

item.title: Harford Mutual VP retires after 35-year career
item.description: Harford Mutual Harford Mutual announced that Vice President Frank P. Kellner III retired on April 1, 2026, after more than 35 years with the company. Outside the company, Kellner has been involved with several community organizations, including Harford Family House, the Harford County Government Local Management Board, and the University of Baltimore’s Risk Management and Insurance Advisory Board.

item.title: Fulcrum acquires Megaphone
item.description: Fulcrum Inc has acquired Megaphone , bringing its co-founders into the company as part of the deal. The pair previously built Megaphone, a platform focused on onchain marketing campaigns that scaled to more than five million users and supported brands including Coinbase and Forbes. Megaphone developed tools for launching tokens and NFTs, collecting user data, and analyzing onchain identity and engagement.

item.title: Goosehead names new general counsel and corporate secretary
item.description: Goosehead Insurance has named Martin Thornthwaite as general counsel and corporate secretary, effective March 30, 2026, replacing John O’Connor, who is leaving the company. Thornthwaite joins from RealPage, where he served as senior vice president and associate general counsel, overseeing litigation, regulatory matters, investigations, employment issues, and corporate governance. Goosehead is a personal lines insurance agency that works with more than 200 carriers and distributes products through corporate and franchise locations across the US.

item.title: Arch Insurance UK Regional Division appoints Head of Casualty
item.description: Arch Insurance UK Regional Division announced the promotion of Mark McGrady to Head of Casualty – UK Regional Division, effective immediately. In this role, he is responsible for leading the expansion of Arch’s casualty portfolio across its UK regional network, leading the underwriting strategy, product development and distribution of its casualty solutions. His appointment reflects our continued commitment to underwriting complex casualty business and he will drive the growth of our product-led liability portfolio, strengthening our presence by delivering innovative, customer-focused solutions.” – Stuart Danskin, Director of Underwriting, UK Regional Division.

item.title: Cyclone Vaianu batters New Zealand’s North Island
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Specialist MGA incubated by Mission launches
item.description: Elitium Specialty , a specialist MGA incubated by Mission Underwriting has launched. “Elitium embodies exactly what Mission was built to support: experienced underwriters creating specialist MGAs with a clear, long-term vision, expertise, and a drive to deliver for their partners and clients. I am confident that Leah and Linda’s thoughtful underwriting and deep experience in this specialist area will set them apart.” – Ross Dingwall, CEO of Mission.

item.title: Novacore launches aerospace segment
item.description: Novacore is expanding into aviation and space risks with the launch of an aerospace segment, adding new underwriting capabilities for complex exposures. “Launching our Aerospace segment is a natural next step as we continue to build out our specialty platform with best-in-class underwriting talent. Rob and Tom each bring exceptional technical expertise and market relationships, and we are committed to building this segment with the same focus on underwriting discipline and sustained profitability that defines our platform.” – Aaron Miller, Chief Executive Officer of Novacore.

item.title: Rokstone adds senior marine underwriter in Singapore
item.description: Rokstone Underwriting has appointed Jackie Lan as Senior Underwriter in its Singapore marine team. Lan joins from QBE Insurance and brings more than 15 years of experience in marine hull and liability underwriting across Asia and the Middle East. The hire supports Rokstone’s expansion in the region following its Lloyd’s capacity deal in 2025.

item.title: Manulife backs dementia prevention clinic in Quebec
item.description: Manulife Canada has committed $1 million over four years to support a dementia prevention clinic at the Douglas Mental Health University Institute. “As the first clinic of its kind in Quebec, this initiative represents an important step forward for dementia prevention in the province. That’s why we’re investing in earlier, evidence-based prevention through the Douglas Cognitive Health and Prevention Clinic powered by Manulife, reflecting our commitment to longevity leadership and to helping Canadians live more years in better health.” – Naveed Irshad, President and CEO, Manulife Canada.

item.title: Keystone names Marc Harris as National Sales Leader
item.description: Insurance brokerage platform Keystone Agency Partners has named Marc Harris as its new National Sales Leader. I have complete confidence in Marcus to continue Arnold Insurance’s legacy of excellence as I focus on this next chapter with KAP.” – Marc Harris, National Sales Leader at Keystone. In this position, Harris will be responsible for overseeing and implementing organic growth strategies with Keystone’s Platform Partners across the company’s national footprint.

item.title: Hippo Builds Unified Claims Platform with Embedded AI
item.description: Hippo (San Jose, Calif.) is reflecting that shift in a unified, AI-enabled claims platform designed to support the full lifecycle of a claim while changing how work is distributed between systems and adjusters. “We’ve delivered a new unified claims platform that serves as a single operating system for our adjusters, a single source of truth for claims data,” says Kyle Ramsay, Chief Product Officer, Hippo. The platform provides “end-to-end AI assistance across the claims lifecycle, from intake to flagging to document generation and more,” Ramsay says, describing a system with AI embedded directly into workflow rather than deployed as a separate tool.

item.title: Ceasefire’s limited lifeline for insurers
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Standard Life launches private markets pension default
item.description: Standard Life has launched Future Opportunities, a new workplace pension default strategy that gives defined contribution members access to private markets as part of their retirement savings mix. The offering is available for trust-based products and is positioned as an alternative to the company’s existing Sustainable Multi Asset default, with a long-term target of around 25% in private market assets. The strategy will gradually add private equity, real assets, private debt, and venture capital, alongside public market holdings, with private markets specialist Future Growth Capital supporting asset selection and portfolio construction.

item.title: DXC Launches AI Workflow Apps to Streamline P&C Operations
item.description: Source: DXC.) DXC Technology (Ashburn, Va.) has introduced a suite of AI-driven workflow applications designed to help insurers modernize operations and reduce reliance on manual processes. “ By bringing together ServiceNow’s agentic AI and workflow technology and DXC’s deep insurance industry expertise, we’re redefining what’s possible in insurance operations ,” says Michael Park, SVP, Global Partnerships and Channels, ServiceNow. The Assure Smart Apps—which the company unveiled at its DXC Connect Insurance Executive Forum—are modular, pre-built applications that integrate with core systems to support claims, underwriting and customer engagement workflows, with implementation timelines of 12 weeks or less.

item.title: Munich Re Integrates Sixfold AI into Realytix Zero Platform
item.description: Munich Re (Munich) has integrated underwriting AI from Sixfold into its Realytix Zero platform to enhance automation across underwriting workflows. “Our partnership with Sixfold reflects the direction underwriting technology is taking,” says Florian Niklas, Co-Founder, Realytix Zero and Head, Underwriting Technologies, Munich Re. “By combining Realytix Zero’s product development and workbench capabilities with Sixfold’s risk analysis, insurers can get products into the market quickly, reduce manual review, and make underwriting decisions faster and more consistently.

item.title: ACORD Study Highlights Persistent M&A Execution Risk for Carriers
item.description: ACORD (New York) has found that nearly one-third of global carrier mergers and acquisitions destroy shareholder value, underscoring ongoing execution challenges despite shifting deal strategies. “For transactions that fell short, value destruction was driven primarily by execution, not deal logic,” says Dave Sterner, SVP, Research and Development, ACORD. The study, Carrier Mergers & Acquisitions: Drivers, Implications & Outcomes , which examined nearly 500 transactions across 84 countries between July 2023 and December 2025, found that 68 percent of deals created value, while 32 percent resulted in value destruction, based on total shareholder return relative to the MSCI World Index.

item.title: PacifiCorp Case Appellate Ruling Could Jeopardize $1B in Oregon Wildfire Victim Damages
item.description: An Oregon appeals court sided with PacifiCorp on Wednesday in a ruling that could jeopardize over $1 billion in damages for victims of the state’s devastating 2020 wildfires. In the ruling, a three-judge appellate panel found that the trial court erred in instructing the jury that it could assume that the evidence presented at trial regarding four different wildfires applied to all class members. The Oregon Court of Appeals sent the class-action case back to a lower trial court over concerns about a jury instruction given during a 2023 trial.

item.title: Meta Pulls Ads Aimed at Recruiting Plaintiffs for Social Media Addiction Lawsuits
item.description: Meta Platforms said on Thursday it is pulling ads from Facebook and Instagram aimed at recruiting new plaintiffs for ongoing litigation accusing it and other social media companies of designing their platforms to be addictive to young users. More than 3,300 lawsuits involving addiction claims are pending in California state court against Meta, Google, Snapchat parent Snap Inc., and ByteDance, TikTok’s parent company. The state court cases largely involve individuals suing the companies over claims that addiction to social media caused mental health harms.

item.title: Strong El Nino, Warmer Sea Impacts Atlantic Hurricane Season Forecasts
item.description: Moderate to strong El Niño events generally have a stronger tropical Atlantic vertical wind shear impact, and a warm Atlantic favors an above-average season, since a hurricane’s fuel source is warm ocean water, according to CSU researchers. Two new hurricane predictions forecast widely different storm totals for the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season. Their April prediction comes with the caveat that it is “historically less accurate compared to those that follow in each season due to the considerable changes that can occur in the atmosphere and ocean between April and the peak of the Atlantic hurricane season from August to October.” Arizona State University’s College of Science Hydrology & Atmospheric Sciences Department, on the other hand, predicts nine hurricanes, four major hurricanes, and 20 named storms.

item.title: The Week’s 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: SiFive Leads With $400M For Custom Chip Designs As Aviation, Biotech And Defense Startups Also Raise Big
item.description: While no billion-dollar rounds led this week’s list, we nonetheless saw a variety of startups in industries ranging from semiconductors to aerospace to biotech raise sizable rounds. The week’s biggest deal was $400 million for SiFive, a semiconductor startup challenging incumbent Arm Holdings with chip designs built on an open rather than proprietary standard. This is a weekly feature that runs down the week’s top 10 announced funding rounds in the U.S.

item.title: Climate conditioned ILW strategy can outperform: Reask / LGT ILS Partners research
item.description: According to a new working paper by catastrophe modelling specialist Reask and specialist insurance-linked securities investment manager LGT ILS Partners, a climate-conditioned Industry Loss Warranty (ILW) strategy can materially outperform traditional static modeling approaches. With this, Francesco Comola, Head of Analytics at LGT ILS Partners, along with colleagues at the firm whilst working alongside Bolliger and Reask’s CEO, Jamie Rodney, developed a climate-conditioned catastrophe modelling framework that combined two key components. The firms conducted a systematic back-test of a climate-conditioned strategy across a portfolio of 36 regional ILWs, spanning a 40-year period from 1985 to 2024.

item.title: Cat wind E&S rate decreases of 20%–30% becoming common amid capacity, competition: CRC
item.description: The catastrophe wind market is one of the most aggressively softening segments within the excess and surplus (E&S) lines property market, with rate decreases in the 20% to 30% range becoming “increasingly common”, according to wholesale insurance services provider CRC Group. For instance, rate decreases in the 20% to 30% range are increasingly common, making CAT wind one of the most aggressively softening segments within the property market,” CRC Group said. In its 2026 Property State of the Market report, CRC Group notes that the cat wind market remains highly competitive, with ample capacity, resulting in continued rate softening across most geographies.

item.title: Aon integrates new AI benchmarks into Radford McLagan Compensation Database
item.description: As demand for artificial intelligence (AI) talent and roles intensifies across industries, global insurance and reinsurance broking group Aon has launched enhancements to its Radford McLagan Compensation Database. By expanding the Radford McLagan Compensation Database with new AI job families and enhanced analytics, we’re helping leaders ground pay decisions in current, defensible market data — even as the market continues to evolve.” AI is rapidly changing work, demanding new skills that blend technical expertise with strategy, governance, risk, and operations. Based on insights from the Radford McLagan Compensation Database, core AI roles such as machine learning engineers, applied data and research scientists and AI platform engineers are among the fastest growing roles in the market, alongside rising demand for adjacent skills such as data engineering and cybersecurity.

item.title: Liberty Mutual establishes $600m endowment to support long-term community resilience
item.description: Sumar leads the Foundation’s long-term vision and strategy, and reports into Francis Hyatt, Chief Community Investments & Sustainability Officer, Liberty Mutual Insurance. “We have long believed that we have a responsibility to our communities and this endowment allows our commitment—and our impact—to continue for generations.” With the creation of the endowment, Liberty Mutual Foundation can expand its resources over time, enhance its ability to address emerging needs more effectively, and pursue multi-year, place-based, collaborative initiatives that drive meaningful, lasting outcomes. Liberty Mutual Insurance has announced the establishment of a $600 million endowment for Liberty Mutual Foundation to build stronger and more resilient communities.

item.title: Swiss Re’s shareholders elect Jean-Jacques Henchoz as new Board member and approve dividend
item.description: Jean‑Jacques Henchoz, the former Chief Executive Officer of large European reinsurer Hannover Re, has been elected as a new member of the Board of Directors of Swiss Re, another of Europe’s big four reinsurance companies. “I would like to thank our shareholders for their continued support and confidence in the Board of Directors, and congratulate Jean-Jacques Henchoz on his election to the Board of Directors,” said Swiss Re’s Chairman, Jacques de Vaucleroy. Alongside Henchoz’s addition to the Board, Swiss Re’s shareholders re-elected all proposed members of the Board of Directors, and also all proposed members of the Compensation Committee, each for one term of office.

item.title: Markel Expands in Australia With Office in Perth
item.description: Markel Insurance, the insurance operations within Markel Group Inc., announced the opening of a new office in Perth, Western Australia, with immediate effect. The new office supports Markel’s strategy to build a local operation across Australia, empowering underwriters to service the domestic business with on-the-ground expertise. “The opening of the Perth office reflects our continued focus on profitable growth in Australia.

item.title: Inszone Acquires Oklahoma’s Schuessler
item.description: Inszone Insurance Services announced its expansion in Oklahoma through the acquisition of Schuessler Insurance, Inc., a long-standing independent insurance agency based in Alva, Oklahoma. Founded in 1973 by Harvey Schuessler, the agency began as a small operation run from Schuessler’s dining room before opening its first office in 1975. Over the decades, Schuessler Insurance built a strong reputation for dependable service and deep community relationships.

item.title: Iowa AG Sues Meta Over Alleged Deceptive Practices on Instagram
item.description: Iowa Attorney General Brenna Bird announced she has filed a lawsuit against Meta and several of its subsidiaries, including Instagram and Facebook, for breaking Iowa’s consumer protection laws. The state also asserts that Meta designed Instagram to be addictive, particularly to Iowa youth, and that this addiction harms Iowa children by substantially affecting their health. The Attorney General’s Office alleges Instagram represents itself as being safe for Iowa’s children and teenagers, yet there is explicit content readily available.

item.title: Aviva enhances protection offering with AI-driven critical illness underwriting tool
item.description: The post Aviva enhances protection offering with AI-driven critical illness underwriting tool appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Aviva has expanded its AI-powered underwriting summarisation tool to individual critical illness applications, marking a further step in the use of generative AI across protection insurance. With the latest rollout, Aviva becomes the first insurer to apply the technology to critical illness underwriting.

item.title: Insurance Distributors Should Buy Carriers
item.description: Large insurance distributors are poised to acquire carriers as AI and private equity remove traditional barriers. Distributors should seek to acquire carriers and develop fit-for-purpose products and capabilities. Independent agents continue to drive premium growth for carriers, with 60% of life insurance sales and increasing annuity sales (46% through IA/IMO/Independent BD) coming from third-party distribution (TPD), according to LIMRA.
